evals.description: AI-heavy content — high em dash density, AI-tell phrases, low sentence variance

input.text: In today's digital landscape, it's important to note that leveraging AI-powered solutions can truly transform your workflow. By delving into the multifaceted aspects of content creation — which is increasingly crucial — organizations can streamline their processes and foster innovation. Needless to say, this is a game-changer for businesses looking to navigate the complexities of modern marketing. The robust framework we've developed is designed to facilitate seamless integration. It's worth noting that our cutting-edge approach empowers teams to optimize their output. Absolutely, this comprehensive solution will revolutionize how you work.

assertions: Returns valid JSON without a top-level error field
human_score is a number between 0 and 100
human_score is below 60 (content is highly AI-like)
checks object contains em_dashes, ai_tell_phrases, filler_intensifiers, sentence_variance, structural_uniformity, and overused_words fields
em_dashes.severity is 'high' or 'elevated' given the em dash density
ai_tell_phrases.instances array contains objects with phrase, context, and category fields
Output contains no rewrites, edits, or suggestions — detection only

evals.description: Clean human-sounding content — should score high

input.text: Last week I broke production. Not intentionally, but here's what happened: I pushed a migration without testing the rollback path. Forty-five minutes later, our support queue had 200 tickets. We fixed it, but I learned more in that afternoon than in the previous six months of incident-free deploys. The lesson wasn't 'test more' — I already knew that. It was that I'd stopped treating rollback as a first-class concern.

assertions: Returns valid JSON without a top-level error field
human_score is a number between 0 and 100
human_score is above 70 (content reads as human-written)

evals.description: Text under 50 words — returns structured error
assertions: Returns JSON with an error field
Does not attempt scoring on insufficient input
